prompts.text: Explain to your client what a motion in limine is before tomorrow's hearing.
Explain to a first-time deponent what a privilege objection actually means.
Explain to your client why a summary judgment ruling tomorrow ends the case.
Explain to a board member what a derivative shareholder suit puts at risk.
Explain to your client what an indemnification carve-out does in their term sheet.
Explain to a paralegal what a Daubert challenge will look like Friday morning.
Explain to your client why we're filing a 12(b)(6) instead of answering the complaint.
Explain to a hostile board what attorney-client privilege does and does not protect.
Explain to an anxious client what voir dire on Monday actually looks like in the room.
Explain to your client why pleading the Fifth in a parallel civil deposition is risky.
Should we accept the $4M settlement offer that landed an hour ago?
Did the motion to compel work?
Is the deposition transcript usable at trial, yes or no?
Should we file the emergency TRO before the close of business today?
Did the expert hold up under cross this afternoon?
Should our client take the plea on the table or push for trial?
Is the jury leaning our way after opening, yes or no?
Should we waive the conflict and stay on the deal?
Did the in-house team's privilege log hold up at the hearing?
Should the GC sign off on the indemnity cap the sellers are demanding?

Explain to a juror in closing how circumstantial evidence builds a case.
Explain to your client what a representations and warranties insurance policy actually buys them.

Explain to your client how earn-out triggers behave like a delayed second contract.
Explain to a jury why a chain-of-custody break does what a broken seal on a package does.

What's the one weakness opposing counsel will hit at oral argument?
Why did the jury believe their witness over ours?

What's the one defense theory the jury is most likely to accept?
Why should the partner approve the discovery motion before tomorrow?
What's the single biggest reason your associate's brief needs another pass?
Why should we walk away from this client engagement after three years?
What's the one piece of evidence the prosecution cannot survive losing?
Why is settling Monday the right call even though we'd win at trial?
Explain the difference between a motion to dismiss and summary judgment to your client.

Settle this morning or push to verdict next week?
File the motion today or wait until after the deposition?
